 {"id":"bd-3rw.2","title":"SECURITY.md: security model","description":"Security model documentation.

Goal: Document FrankenLibC's security guarantees formally.

Document Sections:

1. Threat Model
   - In-scope threats (libc-level memory safety)
   - Out-of-scope threats (kernel, application logic)
   - Attacker capabilities assumed

2. Safety Guarantees
   - Temporal safety: generation counters, P(detection)=1
   - Spatial safety: fingerprints, canaries, P(miss)<=2^-64
   - Galois connection: formal property
   - Lattice monotonicity: formal property

3. Healing Actions
   - ClampSize: when, what, risks
   - TruncateWithNull: when, what, risks
   - IgnoreDoubleFree: when, what, risks
   - QuarantineStale: when, what, risks
   - ReturnSafeDefault: when, what, risks

4. Mode Comparison
   - Strict: compatibility focus
   - Hardened: safety focus
   - Trade-offs and selection criteria

5. Audit Trail
   - Metrics format
   - Evidence ledger structure
   - Forensic analysis guide

6. Limitations
   - Application-internal bugs
   - Kernel vulnerabilities
   - Side-channel considerations
   - Overhead considerations

7. Formal Properties
   - Mathematical foundations
   - Proof sketches
   - Assumptions

Success Criteria:
- Threat model clearly defined
- Guarantees stated with formal bounds
- Limitations explicitly documented
- Security engineer sign-off","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.672969177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:46.316796197Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1m5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:46.316745993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3rw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.672969177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3rw.3","title":"Documentation source-of-truth map + ownership workflow","description":"Background:
- Documentation quality depends on explicit source-of-truth mapping to code/reports.

Scope:
- Define documentation ownership map for README/ARCHITECTURE/DEPLOYMENT/SECURITY/API/TROUBLESHOOTING.
- Link each section to generated artifacts and update triggers.

Deliverables:
1) Doc source-of-truth map.
2) Generation/update workflow.
3) Ownership + review policy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every major doc section traces to maintained evidence sources.
- Update responsibilities are explicit.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for mapping/validator utilities.
- E2E docs-generation dry run.
- Logs: trace_id, doc_section, source_artifact, freshness_status, owner.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.658318980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.658318980Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","docs","governance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.658318980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3rw.4","title":"API/support documentation generation from live project artifacts","description":"Background:
- API/support docs drift quickly unless generated from support and conformance artifacts.

Scope:
- Implement API behavior doc generation from support matrix, conformance outputs, and replacement profiles.
- Include strict/hardened semantics and support-state annotations.

Deliverables:
1) API doc generation pipeline.
2) Symbol-level behavior/support pages.
3) Validation checks for missing or stale entries.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implemented/claimed symbols have generated docs.
- Drift between docs and support artifacts is detectable.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for generator/renderers.
- E2E docs generation + validation run.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, support_state, doc_status, source_refs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.799393720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:31.763669456Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","docs","support-matrix"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.799393720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3rw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3rw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:31.763606679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-3rw.5","title":"Executable docs validation + CI drift enforcement + release-note hooks","description":"Background:
- Deployment/security/troubleshooting docs must be validated as executable guidance.

Scope:
- Add verification scripts that execute documented workflows (deploy, security checks, troubleshooting playbooks).
- Add CI drift gate enforcing doc freshness and executable examples.

Deliverables:
1) Executable doc verification scripts.
2) CI doc-drift gate.
3) Release-note automation hooks tied to closed beads.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Documented critical workflows execute successfully in validation environment.
- Stale or invalid docs fail CI.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for doc-check helpers.
- E2E doc-validation pipeline run.
- Logs: trace_id, doc_workflow, check_result, stale_sections, remediation_hint.","notes":"Planning addendum (from code audit):

Doc-drift/executable-doc validation must also cover *semantic claim drift*, not just runnable commands.

Required claim-drift checks:
1. README membrane healing actions list must match `frankenlibc-membrane::HealingAction` exactly.
   - Current mismatch: README mentions `QuarantineStale`, but `heal.rs` does not define it.
   - Action: either implement the action (with tests + evidence logging) or remove/rename it in docs; add CI drift gate so this cannot regress.

2. Feature contract docs must match compile-time reality.
   - `frankenlibc-membrane` currently refuses to compile without `runtime-math-production` (via `compile_error!`).
   - Docs must explicitly state default production feature set vs research opt-ins, and CI must validate the production controller manifest.

3. FEATURE_PARITY “runtime_math is live” claims must be mechanically checkable.
   - Add/extend a controller manifest + linkage tests (hook points, invocation counters, and decision influence) so “present in code” != “active in runtime” cannot slip through review.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.951687455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:48:21.752940307Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","docs","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.951687455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3rw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3rw.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:31.904787748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3rw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:32.708800792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3tc","title":"Change-point drift policy: BOCPD integration for regime-shift-aware control routing","description":"Critique mapping: #5.

Deliverables:
- Integrate Bayesian change-point detection into routing policy updates.
- Define freeze/escalate policy under detected regime shifts.

Acceptance:
- Synthetic drift fixtures trigger expected regime transitions.
- Non-drift runs remain stable (low false positive rate under budget).

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 validation pass (BrownCompass): BOCPD drift-policy artifact and gates are complete/consistent. Verified scripts/check_changepoint_drift.sh (PASS) and cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness --test changepoint_drift_test -- --nocapture (7 passed). Coverage includes BOCPD parameter consistency, routing escalation policy, monitor integration, false-positive control targets, and summary checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCompass","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:11.505694765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:41:18.634331851Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:41:18.634307616Z","close_reason":"BOCPD change-point drift artifact/gate/test suite is complete and re-validated (gate pass + harness test pass).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","critique","drift","math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3tc","depends_on_id":"bd-182","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:39:13.720909859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3tc","depends_on_id":"bd-35a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:39:13.622707983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3tp","title":"Math value proof: A/B benchmark each kept module against simpler baseline","description":"Critique mapping: #5 + extreme optimization discipline.

Deliverables:
- For each retained module, provide before/after metrics and behavioral equivalence checks.
- Document opportunity score (impact x confidence / effort) and keep only score>=2.0 candidates.

Acceptance:
- Retained modules have objective value evidence.
- No retained module survives solely by aesthetic/novelty argument.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): math_value_proof_test (8 pass) + gate script. Score formula consistent, retained modules meet threshold, core/monitor modules match governance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:11.695462569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","close_reason":"Math value proof operational. 8 harness tests pass. check_math_value_proof.sh validates assessments, scores, and retained module thresholds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","critique","math","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3tp","depends_on_id":"bd-24x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:38:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3tp","depends_on_id":"bd-2wp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:38:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3tp","depends_on_id":"bd-f7r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:38:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-4rl","title":"Closure gate: block bead completion without test commands, logs, and evidence links","description":"Goal: enforce quality by policy, not convention.

Deliverables:
- Automated gate that validates required bead evidence before status can move to closed.
- Evidence checklist includes unit, e2e, logs, and (if perf-related) baseline/profile/isomorphism data.
- Human-readable failure diagnostics for missing evidence.

Acceptance:
- Attempting to close a bead without required evidence fails deterministically.
- Gate is applied to all critique-response workstreams.

- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.

 {"id":"bd-4zc","title":"Kernel: Localization chooser (integrate with decide/profile selection)","description":"Wire localization chooser into decide() as a conservative influence.

Rules:
- Localization may suggest Full, but cannot override hard safety gates.
- In strict mode, de-escalations must be justified by other guarantees (e.g., alpha-investing or approachability).

Outputs:
- Snapshot exports chosen arm id + score margins.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:40.233871959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:15:27.214197756Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:15:27.214179222Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4zc","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:06.898603581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4zc","depends_on_id":"bd-bwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:06.821582642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-5fw","title":"Completion Program: Final release-exit contract + deterministic closure protocol","description":"Background:
- Current project state has strong partial coverage and many gates, but final completion is still fragmented across open epics and in-progress subsystem tasks.
- We need one deterministic closure contract so future execution and review do not rely on implied assumptions.

Goal:
- Define and enforce the exact end-state required to claim “finished, correct, and optimized” for the current declared replacement level.

Deliverables:
1) Explicit release-exit checklist for L0 (interpose) with objective pass/fail checks.
2) Staged upgrade requirements for L1/L2 progression (what must be true before claim changes).
3) Artifact bundle spec for every release candidate:
   - conformance reports,
   - reality report,
   - replacement/profile reports,
   - perf baselines + deltas,
   - evidence/log indices.
4) Gate ordering and ownership (which scripts/tests run in what order and why).

Acceptance Criteria:
- A clean checkout can run a single scripted release dry-run and produce a complete pass/fail dossier.
- No “manual interpretation” required to determine readiness.
- Any missing artifact or breached threshold fails deterministically with actionable diagnostics.

Test and Logging Requirements:
- Add unit tests for checklist parsing/validation logic.
- Add end-to-end release-dry-run script tests (strict and hardened where relevant).
- Emit structured run logs including trace_id, mode, gate_name, outcome, errno/exit_code, timing, and artifact paths.

Optimization/Proof Requirements (Extreme Optimization skill):
- Include benchmark baseline references for all release-critical hot paths.
- Require isomorphism proof references for any optimization included in the release window.

Porting/Spec-first Requirements (Porting-to-Rust skill):
- Reference behavior contracts from PLAN/EXISTING/PROPOSED/FEATURE_PARITY docs directly in acceptance checks so closure is specification-backed.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:34.154973750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:07:41.795570251Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","release","roadmap","verification"]}

Porting/Spec-first Requirements (Porting-to-Rust skill):
- Reference behavior contracts from PLAN/EXISTING/PROPOSED/FEATURE_PARITY docs directly in acceptance checks so closure is specification-backed.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:34.154973750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:49:34.997210657Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","release","roadmap","verification"],"comments":[{"id":118,"issue_id":"bd-5fw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning update (Codex):

- Expanded `bd-2uro` with a self-contained North Star + non-negotiable contracts + phase map + proof-obligation index (so we do not need to consult PLAN_TO_PORT_GLIBC_TO_RUST.md for closure intent).
- Added new child `bd-5fw.4` (Proof Obligations Binder) and set `bd-5fw.3` to *block on* it. This is intended to make release-dossier requirements mechanical: theorem -> evidence -> gate mapping.
- Code audit note (from explorer): README’s healing-action list appears to mention `QuarantineStale`, but `frankenlibc-membrane::HealingAction` does not define it. Also, membrane currently refuses to compile without `runtime-math-production` feature. These should be treated as doc-claim drift and covered by `bd-3rw.5` doc drift gates.

Coordination:
- MCP Agent Mail is currently overloaded (`health_level=red`, as of 2026-02-12). Using bead comments for now.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:49:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5fw.1","title":"Closure contract schema v1: objective release invariants by replacement level","description":"Background:
- We need an explicit, machine-checkable definition of "finished" for L0/L1/L2/L3 so release claims are objective.

Scope:
- Define a canonical closure contract schema with versioning.
- Encode required invariants per level: symbol support claims, call-through limits, test gates, perf ceilings, artifact proofs.

Deliverables:
1) `closure_contract.v1` schema (human+machine readable).
2) Level-specific obligation matrix with pass/fail predicates.
3) Mapping from each obligation to a concrete command/artifact path.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Contract can be validated automatically.
- No requirement remains ambiguous or manual-only.
- Any unmet invariant yields deterministic failure reason.

Rationale:
- This creates a stable control plane for all downstream beads and prevents claim drift.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for schema validation and invariant rule parsing.
- E2E dry-run over intentionally broken contracts.
- Structured logs: trace_id, level, invariant_id, check_cmd, result, artifact_ref.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.361423630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46.445449915Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46.445431891Z","close_reason":"Implemented closure_contract.v1 schema + deterministic validator gate + broken-contract e2e tests + structured log enforcement","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["closure","governance","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.361423630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":103,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-5fw.1. Plan: (1) define closure_contract.v1 schema and level obligations in docs, (2) implement machine validator + deterministic diagnostics, (3) add unit tests and broken-contract tests, (4) wire structured release-check logs.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:53:10Z"},{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","author":"Codex","text":"Delivered closure contract schema + validator gate + integration tests.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/closure_contract.v1.json\n- scripts/check_closure_contract.sh\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/closure_contract_test.rs\n- scripts/ci.sh (extended gates now include closure contract gate)\n\nVerification:\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test closure_contract_test -- --nocapture (6 passed)\n- scripts/check_closure_contract.sh (PASS)\n\nContract supports machine-validated level obligations (L0-L3), transition invariant mapping, deterministic failure reasons, and structured JSONL logs with required fields (trace_id, level, invariant_id, check_cmd, result, artifact_ref).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5fw.2","title":"Deterministic gate orchestration DAG with fail-fast + resume semantics","description":"Background:
- A closure contract is only useful if there is one deterministic execution order for all gates.

Scope:
- Define and implement canonical gate order: lint -> unit -> conformance -> e2e -> perf -> docs/reports -> release dossier.
- Implement fail-fast semantics with reproducible retries and explicit resume points.

Deliverables:
1) Gate DAG with required inputs/outputs per step.
2) Runner orchestration spec and implementation notes.
3) Operator runbook for partial reruns without invalidating evidence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Same inputs always produce same gate order and same result.
- Resume behavior is deterministic and auditable.

Rationale:
- Prevents ad-hoc gate sequencing and accidental green builds.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for DAG validity and resume logic.
- E2E tests for fail-fast and resume flows.
- Logs: trace_id, gate_name, prereq_hash, status, duration_ms, resume_token.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.474554818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32.355931474Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32.337574584Z","close_reason":"Implemented release gate DAG artifact + deterministic fail-fast/resume runner + runbook + integration tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["orchestration","release","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.474554818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:14.671238102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-5fw.2 immediately after closing bd-5fw.1. Plan: define deterministic release gate DAG artifact + implement fail-fast/resume orchestrator script + add harness tests for ordering, fail-fast, and resume-token determinism.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:02:04Z"},{"id":109,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","author":"Codex","text":"Delivered deterministic release gate DAG + fail-fast/resume runner + runbook + tests.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/release_gate_dag.v1.json\n- scripts/release_dry_run.sh\n- tests/conformance/release_gate_runner_runbook.md\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/release_gate_dag_test.rs\n- scripts/ci.sh (extended gates now include release dry-run orchestration)\n\nVerification:\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test release_gate_dag_test -- --nocapture (5 passed)\n- scripts/release_dry_run.sh --mode dry-run (PASS)\n\nFail-fast/resume semantics validated with intentional e2e gate failure and deterministic resume token replay (resume_skip for prior gates; restart at failed gate index).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5fw.3","title":"Release artifact dossier spec + integrity validator","description":"Background:
- Final readiness depends on evidence completeness, not just exit codes.

Scope:
- Specify required artifact dossier: conformance outputs, replacement profile, support/reality reports, perf baselines, logs index, decision explainability bundle.
- Build a validator that enforces completeness and integrity (checksums + schema validation).

Deliverables:
1) Artifact dossier manifest format.
2) Integrity validator and missing-artifact diagnostics.
3) Compatibility policy for dossier format evolution.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Missing or malformed artifact blocks release deterministically.
- Dossier can be revalidated independently after generation.

Rationale:
- Ensures future audits can reconstruct exactly why a release was accepted.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for manifest parser and checksum verification.
- E2E tests for success, missing file, and corrupt artifact scenarios.
- Logs: trace_id, artifact_id, schema_version, digest, validation_result.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.591070967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:47:11.699751037Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","diagnostics","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15n.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.448365477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-29b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:06:13.384091700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-30o.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.522937289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-33p.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.238203688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ot.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.632641217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.591070967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:14.770712980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:47:11.699675606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-b5a.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.338694730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
